content:
将下面完整英文文档翻译成准确、自然的简体中文，保留六个章节标题和所有技术边界。译文之后附上至少二十项英中术语表。

Section One — Ownership. A reliable inference service gives every mutable frontier a single owner. The program may delegate mathematical operations to kernels, but kernels do not silently advance request state. This separation makes successful rounds, rejected speculative drafts, cancellation, and prefix restoration observable as complete transitions.

Section Two — Measurement. Loading time, prompt preparation, prefill, decode, and network transport answer different questions. A benchmark should name the interval behind every reported rate. The first generated token belongs to prefill in this engine, while the remaining generated tokens belong to decode, so the token counts must follow the same boundary.

Section Three — Reproducibility. Inputs are frozen before the official run. The request body, resolved sampling parameters, model identity, context capacity, cache format, and raw timing fields are retained. Warm-up requests are labeled and excluded instead of being mixed into the measured repetitions.

Section Four — Long Context. Allocating the maximum cache capacity is not the same as presenting a maximum-length prompt. The engine can keep a 262,144-token allocation while the test varies the actual prepared prompt. This isolates the cost of visible context without changing the memory plan between cases.

Section Five — Speculation. Draft acceptance is workload dependent. Code, prose, translation, and structured records have different local predictability, so both accepted-draft percentage and output tokens per round are necessary. Throughput remains the user-visible result; acceptance statistics explain rather than replace it.

Section Six — Reporting. Publish the complete configuration, repeated measurements, variation, and material limitations. Do not discard a valid slow run, and do not describe an internal timer as client-observed latency. Clear boundaries make an excellent result easier to trust.
